title: quercus rotundifolia var. pilosella
intro: Quercus rotundifolia var. pilosella, also known as the Gall oak or Quercus rotundifolia pilosella, is a variety of the Quercus rotundifolia tree, commonly found in the Iberian Peninsula, with distinctive hairy leaves and small acorns. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are thought to be due to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Quercus rotundifolia var. pilosella are said to include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and promote overall well-being. Quercus rotundifolia var. pilosella is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
